test.observation: 225 passed, 6 skipped, 2 warnings in 43.69s. 6 skips are gated-model tests requiring HF_TOKEN.

typecheck.observation: All checks passed after fixing tensor ** 2 operator to torch.square() in model.py RMSNorm.

fixesAppliedDuringValidation.description: Changed x.float() ** 2 to torch.square(x.float()) to satisfy ty check unsupported-operator rule
Removed dead code: SparseIndexer.score_proj (nn.Linear) was defined but never used in forward(). Updated docstring to note d_model is retained for interface compatibility.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated architecture.md with BitLinear on-the-fly quantization pattern, gamma persistent=False behavior, bias_param naming. Added MLA default F/S pattern, sparse attention memory note, KL divergence direction. Added Dr.LLM soft routing, d_ffn=5504 rationale, WindowedPool zero-padding.
Updated environment.md with ty quirks: tensor ** 2 operator flag, register_buffer type inference note. Renamed section from 'uv 0.9.0 Quirks' to 'ty 0.0.1-alpha.21 Quirks'.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add verification guidance: workers MUST run all three validators (ty check, ruff check, ruff format --check, pytest) listed in services.yaml, not a subset. The mla-indexcache worker ran ruff check but skipped ty check, which would have caught the tensor operator issue.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mla-indexcache handoff shows ruff check and ruff format --check but no ty check command. Feature verificationSteps include 'ty check && ruff check .'.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Remove or clarify 'Use typing.Protocol for component interfaces' from ml-engineer skill step 4. No implementation in the codebase uses Protocol — all use concrete nn.Module classes. This instruction creates confusion.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: bitnet-bitlinear, mla-indexcache, and drllm-routing all use concrete classes. drllm-routing reviewer explicitly noted this mismatch.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the BitLinear bias_param naming convention so future workers don't rename it or expect standard nn.Linear bias attribute.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: bitnet-bitlinear reviewer flagged this as a potential confusion point for downstream features (GGUF export, cross-area integration).

rejectedObservations.observation: register_buffer attributes cause ty type errors in mla.py
rejectedObservations.reason: false-positive: ty check passes on current codebase. The reviewer's claim about 3 ty diagnostics was not reproducible after fixing the tensor ** 2 operator in model.py. The register_buffer pattern does not currently cause ty errors.
already-documented: mission.md explicitly states 'No long-running services needed. This is a batch processing / training pipeline, not a web application.'
